rationale_for_baseline_tier: With a negative GCA screen and no focal neurology, this is most likely PMR — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency — but telehealth cannot examine, palpate temporal arteries, or obtain ESR/CRP, and steroids should be started under supervision. Urgent same-day/next-day in-person review with bloods and supervised steroid initiation (T3), plus fall/dehydration/pressure-area management for a dependent older adult, is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for any GCA feature or focal neurology.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New severe headache, tender scalp/temples, jaw pain on chewing, or any visual change
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicate Giant Cell Arteritis — vision-threatening, needing urgent high-dose steroids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a new severe headache, tender scalp/temples, jaw pain when chewing, or any change in your vision — go to ED or call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Facial droop, one-sided weakness, numbness, or new bladder/bowel changes
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your face droops, one side goes weak or numb, or you lose bladder/bowel control, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A fall, or being unable to keep hydrated/manage transfers safely
escalation_edges.rationale: Immobility with dependence risks falls/dehydration overnight.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she falls, or you can't keep her hydrated and safely transferred, arrange same-day help — call an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety, hydration and pressure-area care; use assisted transfers and aids.
Support the carer and arrange practical help and the in-person review.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like polymyalgia rheumatica, which usually responds well to steroids — but we want an in-person review and blood tests today before starting them, and to be sure the head arteries aren't inflamed (which can threaten sight). Keep her hydrated, help her move safely with assisted transfers and an over-toilet frame, and change her position to protect the skin.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day/next-day in-person GP review with ESR/CRP; use ED/000 for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Go to ED/000 immediately for any headache/scalp tenderness/jaw pain/vision change or focal neurology; otherwise arrange urgent in-person review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the bilateral girdle stiffness with distal sparing, negative GCA/stroke screen, and the need for ESR/CRP and supervised steroid initiation.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassurance/blind steroids with no GCA screen, potentially missing vision-threatening GCA or a neurological emergency.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An overlapping GCA is missed and the patient loses vision irreversibly.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for a screen-negative PMR presentation when urgent in-person review (T3) suffices.
